method: Read-only browser navigation, layout measurement, a client-side directory filter selection, and skip-link activation. No form, subscription, authentication, deployment, or production action was invoked.

directory_fixture_listing.evidence: The directory rendered the search controls and both populated synthetic fixture listings.

directory_mobile_overflow.evidence: At every requested viewport, the document and body scroll widths equaled the client width; no horizontal page overflow was present.

directory_sport_filter.evidence: At 430px, selecting Basketball updated the native Sport control and filtered the two synthetic fixture cards out of the directory. The document and body remained free of horizontal overflow.

fixture_detail_320px_reflow.evidence: At 320px, document and body scroll widths equaled their 305px client widths, and the fixture detail title rendered.

keyboard_skip_link.evidence: A direct activation of the unique Skip to content link on the current staging fixture detail page left the URL fragment empty and focus on BODY; the staging main element has no tabindex. The local release candidate includes a focus-target fix, but this deployed staging version does not.

conclusion: Current staging has sound mobile reflow and working directory filtering, but it fails the skip-link check and therefore cannot close the customer-journey gate. An approved staging deployment of the local focus fix is required before a fresh keyboard test; screen-reader, touch, 200% zoom, constrained-network, error, subscription, and payment journeys also remain required.
